asia.title: Interior Ministry cancels tainted recruitment list
asia.summary: The Interior Ministry's central committee for local government recruitment examinations on Friday ordered the cancellation of the recruitment list involving 5,924 candidates after investigators found irregularities in examination scores.

asia.title: Vendors struggle despite aid plan
asia.summary: The Commerce Ministry's proposed 40-baht khao kaeng scheme was intended to ease living costs. Instead, it has exposed a simple reality in markets across Thailand: many vendors are already selling meals at that price without government support.

asia.title: Thailand the global leader in cross-border purchases

asia.title: China’s missile test from a submarine is no guarantee of regional stability

These five nations are also the permanent members of the UN Security Council. briefing: Global tensions remain high as the US military continues its strikes on Iran, marking a week of consecutive attacks, while in the tech sphere, former White House AI czar David Sacks warns that the US risks losing its AI edge after China's launch of a new model. In Singapore, property changes are afoot on Orchard Road, with some older malls potentially being rebuilt under new ownership, and the HDB has announced that plans for Sunset Way housing are not yet final, with feedback still being considered. Meanwhile, the Straits Times Index may be impacted by global market trends, including a potential slowdown in the US grocery market and shifts in the energy sector, where US firms such as Chevron and ConocoPhillips have signed deals with Iraq.

digests.singapore: The local news in Singapore is currently focused on various issues, including the authenticity of functional drinks and their health benefits, as well as the potential revitalisation of Orchard Road properties under new ownership. Additionally, there are concerns about housing plans in Sunset Way and the importance of mastering mother tongue languages in the education system, as highlighted by Prime Minister Wong. Meanwhile, other news includes the arrest of individuals involved in cheating and shoplifting cases, the career of Chief Justice Sundaresh Menon, and the restructuring of GovTech, which will result in a reduction of roles over the next two years.
digests.us: The US is facing challenges on multiple fronts, including the risk of losing its edge in artificial intelligence to China, as highlighted by former White House AI czar David Sacks, and ongoing military strikes against Iran. Meanwhile, US firms are signing energy and other deals with Iraq, following President Trump's comments on the country's oil, and a vegetable supplier is pulling some products amid a cyclosporiasis outbreak. The US is also engaging with international events, including the World Cup, where Spain is attempting to make history by winning both the men's and women's trophies back-to-back.
digests.asia: Thailand is addressing issues of corruption and economic support, as seen in the cancellation of a tainted recruitment list and the struggles of vendors despite government aid plans, while also seeking to strengthen its economy through increased investment in oil exploration and production, as well as cooperation with China in areas like artificial intelligence. Meanwhile, in the broader Asia-Pacific region, China is making significant strides in technology and military capabilities, including a successful test of an intercontinental-range submarine-launched ballistic missile, and Europe is looking to leverage Chinese tech to boost its own electric vehicle industry. Europe's automotive sector is under threat from Chinese carmakers, prompting policymakers to take action to remain competitive by 2028.
digests.others: International tensions are rising, with Israel launching a strike on a "terrorist cell" in Gaza, killing at least eight people, while Russia is cracking down on anti-war critics, fining and detaining opponents. Meanwhile, climate change is having a devastating impact, with Canadian wildfires spreading smoke across the US, affecting 109 million people, and heat-related deaths in Germany exceeding 5,100 this year. Sports and competitions are also making headlines, with the World Cup final and European Speedcubing Championship underway, and Australian golfer Herbert leading the British Open.
digests.tech: The technology and innovation headlines highlight various developments, including the US grocery slowdown, food safety concerns with iceberg lettuce, and regulatory updates in the aviation and chipmaking industries. Additionally, new products and projects are being introduced, such as an open-source e-reader and a full-stack framework for Rust, showcasing ongoing innovation in the tech sector. The headlines also touch on unique solutions like painting railroad rails white to reduce derailment and a portable cooling system, demonstrating the breadth of technological advancements.
digests.economist: The Economist's analysis highlights the challenges posed by bureaucracy in the US, demographic issues in China, and geopolitical tensions in Asia, where a crackdown on Cambodia's scam industry and shifting alliances are underway. The publication also examines the tech industry, noting SK Hynix's dominance in memory chips and the potential risks of faltering demand, as well as the complexities of brand positioning in the market. Additionally, the articles touch on the implications of great power competition, including the difficulties of achieving sovereign AI and the trend of Asian middle powers drawing closer together in response to an unreliable America.
